LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; exit 1; fi
LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).stdout: PING 192.168.1.2 (192.168.1.2) 56(84) bytes of data.64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=16.1 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=5.35 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=3 ttl=64 time=4.50 ms--- 192.168.1.2 ping statistics ---3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time 2002msrtt min/avg/max/mdev = 4.500/8.640/16.076/5.269 msLAN_REACHABLE:true
LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).stderr: curl: (28) Connection timed out after 10002 milliseconds
Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.
